title: Analysis of US airport
summary: Several airports in North America and Europe have experienced cybersecurity breaches, including the broadcasting of unauthorized messages over public address systems, disruptions to flight information display screens, and system outages. Notable incidents include hacks at Harrisburg, Windsor, Kelowna, Victoria International Airports in Canada, and the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport. The attackers have used valid accounts and exfiltrated data over alternative protocols.

ttps.procedure: Use of valid accounts for accessing airport PA systems and broadcasting unauthorized messages.
Exfiltration of data over alternative protocols, as seen in the broadcasts praising Hamas and criticizing President Trump.

actors.name: Qilin
actors.description: Hacker consortium responsible for multiple confirmed and unconfirmed attacks, including against Western New Mexico University and Hospital Los Madroños in Spain, as well as the 2022 attack on SpiceJet.

actors.name: Cyber Islam
actors.description: Attributed to the hacks at Harrisburg, Windsor, Kelowna, and Victoria International Airports, broadcasting messages praising Hamas and criticizing President Trump.

malware.name: Seedworm
malware.description: Iranian APT group associated with the use of a Python backdoor called Fakeset on the networks of a U.S. airport and non-profit.
